requests.name: GET via query string
requests.notes:
The mock also accepts GraphQL over GET, reading the query (and optional `variables`) from the query string instead of a JSON body. Here the query travels as the `query` param (URL-encoded by Rest Hippo) and returns the same users list.

Prereq: `make mock-up`. Add a second param `variables` with a JSON value to pass variables over GET. Note: Rest Hippo's GraphQL body editor is POST-oriented, so this uses plain query params with No Body rather than the GraphQL body mode.

requests.name: Basic
requests.notes:
Basic
Bearer
API Key
Digest
AWS CLI
NTLM

requests.name: All auth types
requests.notes:
Basic
Bearer
API Key
Digest
AWS CLI
NTLM

requests.name: Relayed (baseline)
requests.notes:
Baseline — confirms traffic actually goes through the forward proxy.

Prereqs: `make mock-up`; Settings ▸ Proxy enabled with URL http://localhost:9999.

The response should carry the header `X-Proxy-Relayed: resthippo-mock`, stamped by the mock forward proxy. This is a plain-HTTP target, so the proxy uses the absolute-URI forward path (not a CONNECT tunnel).

requests.name: Query: users list
requests.notes:
List query returning `[User!]!`.

Prereq: `make mock-up`.

The mock returns a canned two-user list (Alice id 1 / ADMIN / active, Bob id 2 / USER / inactive); the `role` and `active` arguments are accepted but not actually filtered by the mock. operationName is "ListUsers".

requests.name: SOCKS5 tunnel
requests.notes:
Routes through the mock SOCKS5 proxy instead of the HTTP forward proxy.

Prereqs: `make mock-up`; set Settings ▸ Proxy URL to socks5://localhost:9998.

SOCKS relays raw bytes, so there's no `X-Proxy-Relayed` header and X-Proxy-Error is ignored — success confirms the SOCKS path works. For SOCKS auth, start the mock with MOCK_PROXY_USER/MOCK_PROXY_PASS and set matching proxy credentials in Settings (RFC 1929 user/pass).

requests.name: Variables: {{var}} interpolation
requests.notes:
Verifies that {{var}} tokens resolve in the GraphQL panes before the body is serialized (Feature 34, step 2).

The `userId` variable is defined on this GraphQL folder (value 777). Because the mock's `user` resolver echoes `variables.id`, the response `data.user.id` will be "777" — visible proof the token was interpolated. Interpolation works in the Query pane too (e.g. an inline `role: {{someRole}}` enum), not just in Variables.

requests.name: HTTPS via CONNECT tunnel
requests.notes:
HTTPS target → the proxy opens a CONNECT tunnel and blindly relays encrypted bytes.

Because the tunnel is end-to-end, X-Proxy-Error is invisible here (only the plain-HTTP forward path can read it) — success itself proves the tunnel works. The CONNECT acknowledgement carries `X-Proxy-Relayed: resthippo-mock`.

Prereqs: Settings ▸ Proxy enabled with URL http://localhost:9999. Requires the {{baseUrl}} host (DEV environment) to be reachable; swap in any reachable https:// URL to test CONNECT against a different host.

requests.name: Retry: timeout
requests.notes:
`X-Proxy-Error: 2:timeout` makes the forward proxy hang ~1500ms (X-Proxy-Error-Delay) on the 1st delivery then close — surfacing as a timeout — before succeeding on the 2nd.

Prereqs: Settings ▸ Retry enabled with 'retry on timeout' (on by default), Max attempts ≥ 2, and a request Timeout below the delay (e.g. set Settings ▸ Timeout to 1000ms, or raise X-Proxy-Error-Delay above the timeout).

requests.name: Introspection (fetch schema)
requests.notes:
Schema introspection — the same path the editor's "Fetch schema" button uses to power query autocomplete.

Prereq: `make mock-up`.

Any query containing `__schema` (or named `IntrospectionQuery`) makes the mock return its canned schema: types Query/Mutation/User, enum Role {ADMIN, USER, GUEST}. Tip: instead of sending this, click "Fetch schema" in the GraphQL body toolbar to enable autocomplete in the Query pane.

requests.name: Error: unknown field
requests.notes:
Exercises the GraphQL error path. `unknownField` matches none of the mock's root fields (user / users / createUser), so it returns the GraphQL-conventional HTTP 200 with an `errors` array ("Cannot query the requested field on type \"Query\".") and no `data`.

Prereq: `make mock-up`. Confirms the response viewer surfaces a 200-with-errors response correctly, not just transport errors.

requests.name: Retry: connection reset
requests.notes:
`X-Proxy-Error: 2:reset` makes the forward proxy abruptly close the connection on the 1st delivery — a connection error — then succeed on the 2nd.

Prereqs: Settings ▸ Retry enabled with 'retry on connection error' (on by default), Max attempts ≥ 2.

Exercises the connection-error retry condition rather than a status code.

requests.name: Query: user by id
requests.notes:
Core single-object query with a variable and a named operation.

Prereq: `make mock-up`.

The mock's `user` resolver echoes back `variables.id`, so the response `data.user.id` will be "42" (name Alice, role ADMIN). operationName is auto-derived as "GetUser" from the named query. Change the `id` variable to see it flow through.

requests.name: Proxy auth (Basic 407)
requests.notes:
Exercises proxy authentication on the forward proxy.

Start the mock with credentials:
  MOCK_PROXY_USER=alice MOCK_PROXY_PASS=s3cret make mock-up

The forward proxy then answers 407 Proxy Authentication Required until a matching Basic Proxy-Authorization arrives. Set Settings ▸ Proxy username/password to alice / s3cret to succeed; clear them to observe the 407 (which also carries `X-Proxy-Relayed: resthippo-mock`).

requests.name: Error: missing query (400)
requests.notes:
Sends a body with variables but an empty `query`, producing `{"query":"","variables":{"id":"1"}}`. The mock rejects it with HTTP 400 and an `errors` array ("no GraphQL query provided").

Prereq: `make mock-up`. Demonstrates the non-200 GraphQL error path (vs. the 200-with-errors case in "Error: unknown field"). Type a query into the Query pane to turn this into a success.

requests.name: Invalid scheme (negative)
requests.wsMessage: should never send — connect must fail

requests.name: Retry: exhausted (gives up)
requests.notes:
`X-Proxy-Error: 10` fails the first 9 deliveries (502). With Max attempts < 10 the request exhausts its retries and surfaces the error; the Console shows every attempt.

Prereqs: Settings ▸ Retry enabled, 502 in retry status codes, Max attempts < 10.

Gotcha: the per-URL counter only resets on a success, so each exhausted run leaves the count partway up — after enough runs it can eventually reach 10 and pass. Lower the header value or raise Max attempts to drive it to success deliberately.

requests.name: Custom handshake headers
requests.wsMessage: headers were sent on the upgrade

requests.name: Mutation: createUser
requests.notes:
Mutation with variables and an enum argument.

Prereq: `make mock-up`.

The mock's `createUser` echoes back `variables.name` and `variables.role` (defaults "New User" / "USER" if omitted) and returns the new user with id "100", active true, email derived from the name. operationName is "CreateUser".

requests.name: Retry: custom 503 status
requests.notes:
`X-Proxy-Error: 2:503` makes the forward proxy return 503 for the 1st delivery, then succeed on the 2nd.

Prereqs: Settings ▸ Retry enabled with 503 in the retry status codes (Max attempts ≥ 2).

Demonstrates retrying a specific opted-in status code (vs. the default 502). Change the value after the colon to test other codes.

requests.name: xml
requests.bodyText:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<book>
  <title>The Great Gatsby</title>
  <author>F. Scott Fitzgerald</author>
</book>

requests.name: Retry: 502 then succeed
requests.notes:
The forward proxy returns 502 Bad Gateway for the first 2 deliveries of this URL, then forwards the 3rd upstream.

Prereqs: Settings ▸ Retry enabled, Max attempts ≥ 3, and 502 in the retry status codes.

The per-URL fault counter resets after a success, so this is repeatable. Watch the response Console for the attempt count.

requests.name: Bypass list (direct connect)
requests.notes:
Verifies the no-proxy bypass list.

Add `127.0.0.1` (or `localhost`) to Settings ▸ Proxy bypass list. The request then connects directly to the mock API, ignoring the proxy — so the response should NOT carry `X-Proxy-Relayed`.

Remove the host from the bypass list and the header reappears, confirming traffic is back on the proxy. ({{mockUrl}} is http://127.0.0.1:8888.)

requests.name: plain
requests.bodyText:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<book>
  <title>The Great Gatsby</title>
  <author>F. Scott Fitzgerald</author>
</book>
